summary: The team is sprinting on two major content launches—the China Going-Global report and the Intelligent China program—while simultaneously redesigning the knowledge website and prioritizing SEO for Asia Star, with urgent tasks this week on design feedback, conversion tracking, and report translation.
summaryDetail: The biggest initiative right now is the 'New Chinese Playbook for Going Global' report, a joint CKGSB-IE project launching in Singapore in September. The team is translating the bulk of the Chinese report into English, preparing a one-pager proposal, and exploring op-eds from leadership and alumni. Felix is meeting with Tech in Asia this week to discuss a potential launch event. Separately, the Intelligent China program webpage must go live this week, with traffic driving starting in July; the team is deciding between AI and traditional campaign routes. The knowledge website redesign is in full swing: Joseph and Julie need to mark feedback on Joker's design and share HTML files, with a decision to bring indices to the homepage and display more articles. For SEO, Joseph must provide a brief on conversion tracking and draft initial search areas, while the team analyzes Asia Star participant profiles. Urgent this week: finalize the Shun Tian Shu press release (incorporating 10 points), adjust program figures in the school brochure, and follow up with Carnegie and Impactable agencies. The social media agency search has been resolved by prioritizing Media Minds. Upcoming: the EuroCham event blurb needs tweaking, and the Global Programs brochure feedback must be passed to Midori.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed Joker's and Joseph's design proposals for the knowledge website homepage, noting that Joker's version is too similar to the current site. They discussed combining elements from both to show more articles and bring indices to the front.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Meeting to discuss SEO strategy for CKGSB programs, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs, with a focus on content optimization and conversion tracking.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, brochure updates, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and prioritizing Media Minds for social media.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, titled 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global', to be launched in Singapore in September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), reviewed results, and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with a December 1-4 module in Beijing.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

decisions.text: Videos will not have their own category; they will be dispersed by topic.
Indices (BCI, BSI) should be brought to the knowledge homepage.
The homepage should display more articles, with a pinned article at top left and latest articles below.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; US programs on hold initially.
Intelligent China webpage to be ready this week; start driving traffic from July.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, either incorporating 10 points or turning into 10-point article.
Prioritize Media Minds as social media agency due to small budget and time constraints.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, distributed at events in Singapore.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Target completion: early September for digital version.
Asia Start campaign will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program will be marketed as a deeper dive compared to Asia Start to avoid cannibalization.
Use the editorial design direction for Knowledge revamp.
Use Flow for design of China Going Global report for more polished output and flexibility.
